findings.description: Identity snapshots receive no semantic validation. The compiler accepts duplicate canonical_collection_id values and conflicting bindings that map the same chain identity to different collections, then hashes them into a signed normative object.
findings.failure_mode: Consumers cannot deterministically resolve the authoritative collection identity. Different iteration or indexing strategies may select different bindings, causing attribution to the wrong collection or overwriting one binding with another.
findings.suggested_fix: Add identity_snapshot validation that enforces uniqueness of canonical_collection_id and of the canonical chain tuple (chain_family, chain_id, canonical_address), plus any required alias-disjointness rules. Reject conflicting bindings before canonicalization.

findings.description: Provider identity uniqueness is not checked, and maximumIndependentProviderSet ignores provider_id. Multiple records with the same provider_id but different claimed operators/control domains can therefore satisfy a quorum as if they were distinct providers.
findings.failure_mode: A network policy can pass compilation with required_provider_quorum=2 even though both quorum entries identify the same provider. Runtime finality decisions may then be authorized without the required number of independent sources.
findings.suggested_fix: Require provider_id uniqueness within each network before calculating independence. Also reject conflicting reuse of a provider identity across operator or control-domain claims.

findings.description: Identity effective-status records are signed without validating their time interval. evaluated_at may be equal to or later than expires_at, including for READY status.
findings.failure_mode: The compiler can produce an authoritative identity snapshot that declares READY evidence which was already expired when evaluated. Consumers trusting the signed status can serve stale or invalid identity truth.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate every binding's effective_status so expires_at is strictly later than evaluated_at, and enforce any status-specific evidence and expiry invariants before signing the normative object.
